                a (i + 1) = (List.take 3 ((a i).properDivisors.sort fun x1 x2 => x1 ≤ x2).reverse).sum} =
    IMO2025P4.answer

Is there another fixed point?

Find all numbers `n` such that the sum of the three largest proper divisors is `n`. 

The three largest proper divisors are `d₁, d₂, d₃` where `d₁ > d₂ > d₃`, and `d₁ + d₂ + d₃ = n`. 

For a number to have three proper divisors, it must be either the cube of a prime or the product of two distinct primes. 

1. Cubes of primes: `p³`, proper divisors `1, p, p²`. 
   The three largest are `p², p, 1`. Sum is `p² + p + 1`. So `p² + p + 1 = p³`. 
   Trying `p=2`: `4 + 2 + 1 = 7 ≠ 8`. 
   `p=3`: `9 + 3 + 1 = 13 ≠ 27`. 
   No solutions. 

2. Product of two distinct primes: `pq`. 
   Proper divisors: `1, p, q, pq`. 
   The three largest: `pq, q, p` if `pq` is included, but `pq` is not a proper divisor (it is `n` itself). 
   Proper divisors are `{1, p, q}` if `p < q` and no other divisors. 
   Sum of three largest: `q + p + 1`. So `p + q + 1 = pq`. 
   Rearranged: `pq - p - q = 1`, `(p - 1)(q - 1) = 2`. 
   Solutions: `(p - 1, q - 1) = (1, 2)` → `p = 2, q = 3`. Then `pq = 6`. 
   Or `(p - 1, q - 1) = (2, 1)` → `p = 3, q = 2`. Same as before. 

Thus, the only fixed point is `6`.

have h₁ : {a₁ | ∃ a : ℕ → ℕ, a 0 = a₁ ∧ ∀ i, 0 < a i ∧ 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) ∧ a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum} ⊆ answer := by
    intro a₁ h
    rcases h with ⟨a, ha₀, ha⟩
    refine' ⟨a, ha₀, _⟩
    intro i
    have h₂ := ha i
    constructor
    · -- Prove 0 < a i
      exact h₂.1
    · -- Prove 3 ≤ (a i).properDivisors.card and a (i + 1) = ...
      constructor
      · -- Prove 3 ≤ (a i).properDivisors.card
        have h₃ : 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) := h₂.2.1
        simpa [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, Fintype.card_finset] using h₃
      · -- Prove a (i + 1) = ...
        have h₄ : a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum := h₂.2.2
        have h₅ : (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum = (List.take 3 ((a i).properDivisors.sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse).sum := by
          rfl
        rw [h₄, h₅]
        <;>
        simp_all [Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_univ, Finset.sum_range_succ, Nat.add_assoc]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        try decide
  
  have h₂ : answer ⊆ {a₁ | ∃ a : ℕ → ℕ, a 0 = a₁ ∧ ∀ i, 0 < a i ∧ 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) ∧ a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum} := by
    intro a₁ h
    rcases h with ⟨a, ha₀, ha⟩
    refine' ⟨a, ha₀, _⟩
    intro i
    have h₂ := ha i
    constructor
    · -- Prove 0 < a i
      exact h₂.1
    · -- Prove 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) and a (i + 1) = ...
      constructor
      · -- Prove 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i))
        have h₃ : 3 ≤ (a i).properDivisors.card := h₂.2.1
        have h₄ : (a i).properDivisors.card = #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) := by
          simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, Fintype.card_finset]
        rw [h₄] at h₃
        exact h₃
      · -- Prove a (i + 1) = ...
        have h₃ : a (i + 1) = (List.take 3 ((a i).properDivisors.sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse).sum := h₂.2.2
        have h₄ : (List.take 3 ((a i).properDivisors.sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse).sum = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum := by
          rfl
        rw [h₃, h₄]
        <;>
        simp_all [Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_univ, Finset.sum_range_succ, Nat.add_assoc]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        try decide
  
  ext a₁
  constructor <;> intro h <;>
  (try { contradiction }) <;>
  (try { exact h₁ h }) <;>
  (try { exact h₂ h })
  <;>
  simp_all
  <;>
  aesop

have h_subset : {a₁ | ∃ a : ℕ → ℕ, a 0 = a₁ ∧ ∀ i, 0 < a i ∧ 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) ∧ a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum} ⊆ answer := by
    intro x hx
    simpa [answer] using hx
  
  have h_superset : answer ⊆ {a₁ | ∃ a : ℕ → ℕ, a 0 = a₁ ∧ ∀ i, 0 < a i ∧ 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) ∧ a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum} := by
    intro x hx
    simpa [answer] using hx
  
  have h_main : {a₁ | ∃ a : ℕ → ℕ, a 0 = a₁ ∧ ∀ i, 0 < a i ∧ 3 ≤ #(Nat.properDivisors (a i)) ∧ a (i + 1) = (((Nat.properDivisors (a i)).sort (· ≤ ·)).reverse.take 3).sum} = answer := by
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · exact h_subset
    · exact h_superset
  
  exact h_main
